name: Tom Perez
subject: Another classic Trump lie
body:
Republicans are back with a new health care "plan."
---
**Add your name to stand with Democrats and say you'll fight to protect our health care:

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Our-Health-Care1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd")**
Friend --

Right before Paul Ryan pulled the GOP's ACA repeal bill, Donald Trump announced that he was planning to give up on trying to take people's health care away.

Turns out that was another classic Donald Trump lie.

This week, the Trump administration and the most extreme wing of the House GOP started negotiating another attempt to eliminate parts of the Affordable Care Act.

On the chopping block this time: maternity care and protections for people with pre-existing conditions.

**[This plan is no better than the last one. Will you let Republicans know you'll fight it with everything you've got?](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Our-Health-Care1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+This+plan+is+no+better+than+the+last+one&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd")**

For Republicans to make it harder and costlier for women and sick people to get the care they need is heartless. Using them as a bargaining chip to pass a bill that would take care away from millions of Americans is disgraceful.

Democrats won't stand for it. We're going to fight back.

Last time, we put up an incredible show of grassroots force that made Republicans think twice about even voting on a health care bill like this. We have to be ready to fight just as hard again.

**That starts today -- sign your name and say you're ready to protect affordable health care:**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Our-Health-Care1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd")**
More to come. Thanks for being with us on this.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c93f/4067b0a/2ec9201bb/7e7385ad/1909020931/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c93f/4067b0a/2ec9201bb/7e7385aa/1909020931/VEsD/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c93f/4067b0a/2ec9201bb/7e7385ab/1909020931/VEsA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c93f/4067b0a/2ec9201bb/7e7385a8/1909020931/VEsB/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170409_tp_nd&source=em_20170409_tp_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: =?UTF-8?Q?Trump=20Headquarters?=
subject: FW: re: defending the President
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican National Committee Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Following up on Newt Gingrich’s message. |

Friend, Since Newt Gingrich emailed you yesterday, things have only gotten worse... Democrats have raised a total of $8.3 MILLION to buy our congressional seat in Georgia’s sixth district. It’s the seat Newt proudly held when he served as Speaker of the House. And if we don’t fight back against this last minute spending blitz, Nancy Pelosi will be one step closer to becoming Speaker of the House again, and derailing all of President Trump’s great progress. That’s why we need another $1OO,OOO by MIDNIGHT to keep our ads running in this must-win district that we’ve held since 1979. With just 9 days until the first big election of 2017, please contribute just $1 to fight back against the Hollywood money flooding into Georgia. Thanks, Trump Headquarters Contribute $1 ---------- Forwarded message ---------- From: Newt Gingrich Date: April 08, 2017 at 10:04 AM Subject: re: defending the President To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, I know your inbox has been flooded with messages about the special election that’s being held in GA-6 on April 18, but I wanted to touch base with you before Election Day. The Democrats are doing everything they can to win this seat for the first time since 1979 and reverse all the progress President Trump has accomplished in his first few months. They’ve brought in big names like Nancy Pelosi and even have liberal Hollywood celebrities on the ground to campaign for their cherry-picked liberal candidate. But, I’m counting on your support today to keep GA-6 in Republican hands. This is our first big test, Friend. We need to keep this seat red and protect the president. With just 10 days until the first big election of Donald Trump’s term, please contribute just $1 to combat the MILLIONS that Democrats are rushing to turn Georgia blue. Contribute $1 Let’s make sure we put a conservative, Pro-Trump voice for GA-6 in the House. Thank you for your support of our party and our President, Next Gingrich Former Speaker of the House Former Representative of GA-6

Contribute $1

name: Team Kasich
subject: The latest
body:
In response to the recent U.S. action in Syria, Gov. John Kasich
issued the following statements.

Peter, in response to the
recent U.S. action in Syria, Gov. John Kasich issued the
following statements:

"The Assad regime committed war
crimes that can not be tolerated. The U.S. response was
appropriate and necessary."

"Assad must go. And whether he is
forced out sooner or later, he must be tried for war crimes. We
should begin that process now."

Gov. Kasich also issued a
statement congratulating Judge Neil Gorsuch on his confirmation
to be the next Associate Justice of the U.S. Supreme Court. His
is a much needed conservative voice on the court.

Earlier this week, Gov. Kasich
continued his tradition of taking his annual State of the State
outside of the State Capitol by visiting the City of Sandusky on
the shores of Lake Erie near where he vacationed as a child.

Speaking before a packed theater
of 1,500 people, he said the state of the state remains strong
and stable as Ohio has created more than 460,000 new private
sector jobs and a more diversified economy. He outlined proposals
to embrace the next generation of technologies that will lead to
the jobs of the future. During his address, he called for
investing up to $20 million to help advance new scientific
breakthroughs that can help curb drug abuse and addiction.

Watch the full speech here:

Read more below:

• Dispatch Editorial: Get
Ready for the Future

• Dispatch Editorial:
Treatment, Research Shouldn't Be at Odds

Also last week, Gov. Kasich
joined Amazon in celebrating the opening of their new fulfillment
center near Columbus. Ohio has been a leader in the nation in
diversifying our economy and attracting companies like Amazon and
others that are creating jobs and opportunities for our labor
force. Read more (here) from the Plain Dealer.

In case you missed the recent
column in The Hill about Gov. Kasich's speech at the Toner Prize
dinner, read more below:

"…His message is one that
needs to be listened to and - most of all - followed.

Talking about his experience in
Congress, Kasich noted that party label was not an obstacle or
impediment to members developing genuine and close friendships.

"We got along," he said. "We
played basketball in the gym. ... We kidded and joked. ... We had
dinner."

And just imagine this one: "We
called our Democratic colleagues on holidays."

He went on to say that nowadays,
compromise is the "dirtiest word." There is "no tolerance."

For good things to happen, Kasich
declared that "we have to listen to what other people say."

Kasich, I believe, does not
believe it's all about winning. He wasn't afraid to say that
"people need more content." He pleaded for citizen participation
in the civic arena: "The public have to come out of their silos."
He described that sentiment "as not Republican or Democratic."

All of these words were not just
phony, insincere pabulum. That evening, it was obvious Kasich was
not just giving a speech; but he was trying to change the world
we now live in…"

"Kasich's mission that night was
an appeal to the best in us. It wasn't your typical pol speech.
He even had the audacity to talk about "our humanness." He
delivered it with a certain joy. He didn't read from a
teleprompter or from a script. The words seem to come from his
head and, may I be permitted to say it? From his heart.

After the speech, he stood in the
center of the room and talked to everybody. It looked like he
didn't want to leave.

Now, maybe Kasich feels
liberated. He's term-limited as governor. A new chapter is
starting for him. Whatever his motivation that night, he made
people think. He called on them to change the political
atmosphere and carry on with purpose.

I now have a far different
opinion of him and I hope he continues to be active and does not
go away.

Kasich is good for the Republican
Party and the country. His voice and his presence are needed -
especially now."

You can read the entire column
(here).

Thank you for your support!

-Team Kasich

Paid for by Kasich
for America
www.JohnKasich.com

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com

clean_body: In response to the recent U.S. action in Syria, Gov. John Kasich issued the following statements. Peter, in response to the recent U.S. action in Syria, Gov. John Kasich issued the following statements: "The Assad regime committed war crimes that can not be tolerated. The U.S. response was appropriate and necessary." "Assad must go. And whether he is forced out sooner or later, he must be tried for war crimes. We should begin that process now." Gov. Kasich also issued a statement congratulating Judge Neil Gorsuch on his confirmation to be the next Associate Justice of the U.S. Supreme Court. His is a much needed conservative voice on the court. Earlier this week, Gov. Kasich continued his tradition of taking his annual State of the State outside of the State Capitol by visiting the City of Sandusky on the shores of Lake Erie near where he vacationed as a child. Speaking before a packed theater of 1,500 people, he said the state of the state remains strong and stable as Ohio has created more than 460,000 new private sector jobs and a more diversified economy. He outlined proposals to embrace the next generation of technologies that will lead to the jobs of the future. During his address, he called for investing up to $20 million to help advance new scientific breakthroughs that can help curb drug abuse and addiction. Watch the full speech here: Read more below: • Dispatch Editorial: Get Ready for the Future • Dispatch Editorial: Treatment, Research Shouldn't Be at Odds Also last week, Gov. Kasich joined Amazon in celebrating the opening of their new fulfillment center near Columbus. Ohio has been a leader in the nation in diversifying our economy and attracting companies like Amazon and others that are creating jobs and opportunities for our labor force. Read more (here) from the Plain Dealer. In case you missed the recent column in The Hill about Gov. Kasich's speech at the Toner Prize dinner, read more below: "…His message is one that needs to be listened to and - most of all - followed. Talking about his experience in Congress, Kasich noted that party label was not an obstacle or impediment to members developing genuine and close friendships. "We got along," he said. "We played basketball in the gym. ... We kidded and joked. ... We had dinner." And just imagine this one: "We called our Democratic colleagues on holidays." He went on to say that nowadays, compromise is the "dirtiest word." There is "no tolerance." For good things to happen, Kasich declared that "we have to listen to what other people say." Kasich, I believe, does not believe it's all about winning. He wasn't afraid to say that "people need more content." He pleaded for citizen participation in the civic arena: "The public have to come out of their silos." He described that sentiment "as not Republican or Democratic." All of these words were not just phony, insincere pabulum. That evening, it was obvious Kasich was not just giving a speech; but he was trying to change the world we now live in…" "Kasich's mission that night was an appeal to the best in us. It wasn't your typical pol speech. He even had the audacity to talk about "our humanness." He delivered it with a certain joy. He didn't read from a teleprompter or from a script. The words seem to come from his head and, may I be permitted to say it? From his heart. After the speech, he stood in the center of the room and talked to everybody. It looked like he didn't want to leave. Now, maybe Kasich feels liberated. He's term-limited as governor. A new chapter is starting for him. Whatever his motivation that night, he made people think. He called on them to change the political atmosphere and carry on with purpose. I now have a far different opinion of him and I hope he continues to be active and does not go away. Kasich is good for the Republican Party and the country. His voice and his presence are needed - especially now." You can read the entire column (here). Thank you for your support! -Team Kasich Paid for by Kasich for America www.JohnKasich.com This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com You were added to the system July 18, 2015.
